qt: Which of the following states that the objects are commodified by acquiring an exchange value instead of having merely an intrinsic use value?
Fritz Haug is a famous _____________.
______________ use the term hegemony in a different manner by explaining the phenomenon as subtler and more pervasive.
Model of ________________ sees media subservient to other institutions, which are themselves interrelated.
_____________ is the domination or rule by one state or nation over another.
The concept of false needs is identified particularly with the work of:
Which of the following is the process by which one owner acquires all aspects of production and distribution of a single type of media product?
Which of the following states that culture industry deals with falsehoods not truths?
Which of the following is the process by which one company buys different kinds of media, concentrating ownership across different kinds of media?
In early 1970s who among the following scholars/ economists documented the concentration and consolidation of ownership in publishing, print, broadcasting, film and recording industries in UK?

According to German Marxist, Fritz Haug, the power of the ______________ industry to use the appearance of products as a means of stimulating desire for them is now a worldwide phenomenon, and has intervened in the imaginations of people through their cultures.
The term ‘mediation of social relations’ mean:
op: The role of government agencies in a society
The role of state-controlled media in a society
The role of newspapers in a society
The role of mass communication in a society

qt: All of the following are the main principles of Marxist theory, EXCEPT:
Which of the following term means separation and distance?
Political economy theories are inherently critical but some _______________ are not.
The term _____________ serves to this long and diverse tradition which owes its origin to the work of group of post 1933 scholars from the Marxist school of Applied social research in Frankfurt.
________________ is/are the institutions and values of a given society.
According to_________________, rule is political and in critical times is based on coercion or force.
Which of the following is manifested through direct force or its threat needed by a state when its degree of hegemonic leadership is low or fractured?
Some cultural and political economy theories are also referred to as _________________ because they openly espouse certain values and use these values to evaluate and criticize the status quo.

Which of the following is an old label that has been revived to identify a socially critical approach which focuses primarily on the relation between the economic structure and dynamics of media industries and the ideological content of media?
According to Marx, capitalism may be able to produce goods and materialist abundance for large numbers of people but it necessarily generates __________________.
Karl Marx developed his theory in the latter part of which of the following century?
Which of the following perspective says that mass media should be interpreted as an instrument to spread and reinforce the dominant hegemony?
According to Marx, ideological domination is the outcome of the relentless logic of the ___________________.

________________ is an essential institution in advanced capitalist societies because it is necessary to motivate people to work hard so that they can accumulate money, which is later used to buy things.
Which of the following arises when individuals voluntarily assimilate the worldview of the dominant group?
Who among the following scientists gave the description of ‘one dimensional man’ to the mass consumption society founded on commerce advertising and spurious egalitarianism?
The Frankfurt School was most closely tied to what aspect of the Marxist project?
op: The argument that political revolution is necessary
The argument that critique of culture is necessary
The argument that scientific methods can be used to determine economic laws

qt: Which of the following believes that culture industry reflects the consolidation of commodity fetishism, the domination of exchange value and the ascendancy of state monopoly capitalism?
All of the following economic processes are said to have played a part in increasing the reach of media corporations EXCEPT:
The positive version of centrifugalism stresses modernization, freedom and mobility as the effects to be expected from media. Which of the following term defines this approach?
A ______________ theory raises questions and provides alternate ways of interpreting the social role of mass media.
The most important members of the ____________ were Max Horkheimer and Theodor Adorno but other including Herbert Marcuse and Walter Benjamin played an important role.
Karl Marx used the term bourgeoisie for ______________ in his theory.

For Marx social change was explained by the struggle between competing and antagonistic forces in society that he called - following the work of another German philosopher, ________________, on the historical development of ideas - the dialectic process.

For Marx social change was explained by the struggle between competing and antagonistic forces in society that he called the _______________, following the work of George Hegel on the historical development of ideas.
Systems can be closed and ___________ or they can be open and dynamically balanced.
________________ focuses on whether media content can have an immediate and direct effect on specific thoughts and actions of individuals.

______________ structural theories focus on how social elites use their economic power to gain control over and exploit to propagate hegemonic culture as a means of maintaining their dominant position in social order.
____________________ interpretive theories focus on how individuals and social groups use media to create and foster forms of culture that structure everyday life.
A/an ______________ system consists of parts that interrelate in such a way that overall system can monitor its environment and adjust to both internal and external changes.
